symbols.name: weightedPick
symbols.detail: [USED] Runtime-imported by 1 file: src/engine.ts | [DUPLICATE] Identical algorithm to pickFromWeighted in src/reels.ts. Both implement cumulative weight method for random selection: reduce weights, generate random value, iterate accumulating weights, return on threshold match, fallback to last item. Completely interchangeable semantically. | [NEEDS_FIX] Two independent defects: non-certifiable RNG for regulated gaming domain; unguarded empty-array path returns undefined as T. | [LEAN] Minimal cumulative-weight algorithm with a single generic type parameter that's genuinely needed for reuse across symbol types. No unnecessary abstractions; the loop is the standard O(n) approach appropriate for a small fixed-size weight array (8 symbols). | [NONE] No test file found. Critical edge cases untested: empty arrays, single item, zero weights, negative weights, weights summing to zero, boundary rolls at exact cumulative thresholds, and statistical distribution correctness. Used by src/engine.ts, making this a production risk. | [PARTIAL] JSDoc describes the function's purpose and algorithm but omits @param descriptions for `items` and `weights`, and no @returns tag documenting the return value or edge-case behavior (e.g., empty arrays, negative weights, mismatched array lengths).

duplicate_target.similarity: 95% identical logic — cumulative weight random selection with matching control flow

findings.detail: Inferred slot-machine domain from Internal Reference Documentation (reels, paylines, scatter, RTP target 95%). `Math.random()` is a non-seeded, non-auditable PRNG — not certifiable for regulated gaming RNG (industry convention). A cryptographically-secure or independently-auditable RNG is required.

When `items` is empty: `totalWeight = 0`, `roll = 0`, the loop body never executes, and `items[items.length - 1]` evaluates to `items[-1]` = `undefined`, returned as type `T`. Similarly, if `weights.length < items.length`, `weights[i]` becomes `undefined` for out-of-bounds indices, making `cumulative` NaN and causing the loop to never return — the fallback then always selects the last item, silently biasing the distribution.

actions.description: Replace Math.random() with a cryptographically-secure or independently-auditable RNG (e.g. crypto.getRandomValues) to meet regulated gaming certification requirements.
Add a guard at function entry: throw (or return a typed sentinel) when items.length === 0 or items.length !== weights.length to prevent undefined-as-T and silent distribution bias.

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 4.5/10 (1 FAIL, 3 WARN, 13 PASS)

rules.detail: No violations detectable in this 16-line file; no implicit-any or loose type inference present.
Generic <T> used correctly throughout; no explicit or implicit any.
No utility-type opportunities missed for a function of this shape.
Both parameters are read-only by design but not declared as such. `items: T[]` and `weights: number[]` should be `readonly T[]` and `readonly number[]`.
No interface/type declarations in this file.
No obvious lint violations. Loop mutation of `cumulative` is intentional and acceptable.
JSDoc block present on the single exported function.
No deprecated APIs or outdated patterns used.
Fully synchronous function; no async concerns.

Slot-machine domain confirmed by .anatoly/docs/ (reels, WILD, SCATTER, jackpot, RTP, free-spin vocabulary throughout the project). `Math.random()` is a non-cryptographic PRNG seeded from a non-auditable source and is not certifiable for regulated gaming RNG under any major jurisdiction (Nevada, Malta GRA, UKGC, etc.). Using it for outcome determination in a wagering product is a compliance and security FAIL. A CSPRNG (`crypto.getRandomValues()` in Node ≥ 15 / Web Crypto API) must be used instead.
O(n) single pass over weights; no unnecessary allocations.

`Math.random()` is called directly inside the function, making deterministic unit tests impossible without monkey-patching globals. Injecting a `randomFn: () => number` parameter (defaulting to `Math.random`) enables both CSPRNG injection in production and seeded-random injection in tests.
No applicable 5.5+ features omitted for this utility.

No guard against empty `items` / `weights` arrays or mismatched lengths. An empty `items` array causes `items[items.length - 1]` to return `undefined` while the return type promises `T`. A mismatched `weights` length silently reads `undefined` array slots as `NaN`, corrupting cumulative arithmetic and biasing the final slot. Both are runtime crashes/silent bugs in a gaming engine.

suggestions.description: Replace `Math.random()` with an injectable CSPRNG function. Fixes both the security/compliance violation (rule 13) and the testability issue (rule 15).

suggestions.after: export function weightedPick<T>(items: readonly T[], weights: readonly number[]): T {
